company_type: Professional Services - IT Services and Consulting

dependencies: Substantial majority of employees located in India (approximately 241,500 employees)
Reliance on global delivery model with coordination between India delivery centers and client locations
Dependence on technology infrastructure and communications systems across global locations
Reliance on third-party alliance partners including Adobe, Amazon Web Services, Cisco, Google, Microsoft, Oracle, Pegasystems, Salesforce, SAP, ServiceNow, Workday
Dependence on cloud service providers and software vendors for service delivery
Reliance on subcontractors for additional capacity (though historically immaterial)
risks: Concentration of operations in India exposes company to country-specific risks including economic volatility, currency fluctuations, regulatory changes

Disruptions to global delivery capabilities from hostilities, political unrest, terrorist attacks, cybersecurity incidents, power/water shortages, telecommunications failures, natural disasters, extreme weather, pandemics
Risk of third-party alliance partners competing with company, forming exclusive arrangements with competitors, or discontinuing services
Performance failures by alliance partners could delay company performance or require alternative arrangements at company cost
Supply chain disruptions from geopolitical developments, international conflicts, trade disputes
Infrastructure and resource availability risks from climate change and extreme weather events
Severe flooding risk in Chennai, India where substantial delivery operations are located

regulatory_risks: Immigration law changes and visa restrictions in United States, United Kingdom, EU affecting H-1B and L-1 visa holders
Anti-outsourcing legislation proposals at federal and state levels in United States
EU Directive of 2014 on intracompany transferees creating new notification and documentation requirements
Incoming U.S. administration's expected immigration agenda creating uncertainty
Data security and privacy laws including California Consumer Privacy Act, California Privacy Rights Act, HIPAA, India Digital Personal Data Protection Act 2023, UK GDPR, EU GDPR
EU AI Act entered into force in 2024 with parts applying beginning 2025
Rapidly evolving AI regulations across jurisdictions with uncertain scope and interpretation
Code on Social Security 2020 in India potentially increasing employment and post-employment benefit costs
India-Mauritius Income Tax Treaty amendment potentially increasing effective tax rate
OECD Pillar Two global minimum tax framework (15% minimum tax)
California climate change disclosure requirements
European Union Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive (CSRD)
Trade controls and sanctions regulations
Anti-corruption laws including FCPA and UK Bribery Act
Anti-money laundering regulations
Environmental and climate change regulations
Government contracting compliance requirements with stricter regulatory standards
Content requirements and trade restrictions across jurisdictions
Tariff regulations
Antitrust laws
Employment and labor relations laws varying by jurisdiction
Human rights regulations
Intellectual property laws including patent, copyright, trade secret protections
Export control regulations
Debarment or suspension risks from government contracting violations

sanction_risks: International tensions including Russia's invasion of Ukraine heightening cyber-threat risks
Conflicts in Middle East creating heightened cybersecurity risks
Trade disputes potentially causing clients to reduce spending
Potential sanctions exposure from operations in various jurisdictions
Trade restrictions and tariffs affecting business operations
